translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ, through the will of God, according to a promise of life that [is] in Christ Jesus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: to Timotheus, beloved child: Grace, kindness, peace, from God the Father, and Christ Jesus our Lord!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I am thankful to God, whom I serve from progenitors in a pure conscience, that unceasingly I have remembrance concerning thee in my supplications night and day,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: desiring greatly to see thee, being mindful of thy tears, that with joy I may be filled,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: taking remembrance of the unfeigned faith that is in thee, that dwelt first in thy grandmother Lois, and thy mother Eunice, and I am persuaded that also in thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For which cause I remind thee to stir up the gift of God that is in thee through the putting on of my hands,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for God did not give us a spirit of fear, but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: therefore thou mayest not be ashamed of the testimony of our Lord, nor of me his prisoner, but do thou suffer evil along with the good news according to the power of God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who did save us, and did call with an holy calling, not according to our works, but according to His own purpose and grace, that was given to us in Christ Jesus, before the times of the ages,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and was made manifest now through the manifestation of our Saviour Jesus Christ, who indeed did abolish death, and did enlighten life and immortality through the good news,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: to which I was placed a preacher and an apostle, and a teacher of nations,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for which cause also these things I suffer, but I am not ashamed, for I have known in whom I have believed, and have been persuaded that he is able that which I have committed to him to guard -- to that day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The pattern hold thou of sound words, which from me thou didst hear, in faith and love that [is] in Christ Jesus;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the good thing committed guard thou through the Holy Spirit that is dwelling in us;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thou hast known this, that they did turn from me -- all those in Asia, of whom are Phygellus and Hermogenes;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: may the Lord give kindness to the house of Onesiphorus, because many times he did refresh me, and of my chain was not ashamed,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but being in Rome, very diligently he sought me, and found;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: may the Lord give to him to find kindness from the Lord in that day; and how many things in Ephesus he did minister thou dost very well know.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou, therefore, my child, be strong in the grace that [is] in Christ Jesus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the things that thou didst hear from me through many witnesses, these things be committing to stedfast men, who shall be sufficient also others to teach;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thou, therefore, suffer evil as a good soldier of Jesus Christ;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: no one serving as a soldier did entangle himself with the affairs of life, that him who did enlist him he may please;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if also any one may strive, he is not crowned, except he may strive lawfully;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the labouring husbandman it behoveth first of the fruits to partake;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: be considering what things I say, for the Lord give to thee understanding in all things.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Remember Jesus Christ, raised out of the dead, of the seed of David, according to my good news,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in which I suffer evil -- unto bonds, as an evil-doer, but the word of God hath not been bound;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because of this all things do I endure, because of the choice ones, that they also salvation may obtain that [is] in Christ Jesus, with glory age-during.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Stedfast [is] the word: For if we died together -- we also shall live together;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: if we do endure together -- we shall also reign together; if we deny [him], he also shall deny us;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: if we are not stedfast, he remaineth stedfast; to deny himself he is not able.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These things remind [them] of, testifying fully before the Lord -- not to strive about words to nothing profitable, but to the subversion of those hearing;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: be diligent to present thyself approved to God -- a workman irreproachable, rightly dividing the word of the truth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the profane vain talkings stand aloof from, for to more impiety they will advance,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and their word as a gangrene will have pasture, of whom is Hymenaeus and Philetus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who concerning the truth did swerve, saying the rising again to have already been, and do overthrow the faith of some;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: sure, nevertheless, hath the foundation of God stood, having this seal, 'The Lord hath known those who are His,' and 'Let him depart from unrighteousness -- every one who is naming the name of Christ.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And in a great house there are not only vessels of gold and of silver, but also of wood and of earth, and some to honour, and some to dishonour:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: if, then, any one may cleanse himself from these, he shall be a vessel to honour, sanctified and profitable to the master -- to every good work having been prepared,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the youthful lusts flee thou, and pursue righteousness, faith, love, peace, with those calling upon the Lord out of a pure heart;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the foolish and uninstructed questions be avoiding, having known that they beget strife,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and a servant of the Lord it behoveth not to strive, but to be gentle unto all, apt to teach, patient under evil,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in meekness instructing those opposing -- if perhaps God may give to them repentance to an acknowledging of the truth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they may awake out of the devil's snare, having been caught by him at his will.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And this know thou, that in the last days there shall come perilous times,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for men shall be lovers of themselves, lovers of money, boasters, proud, evil-speakers, to parents disobedient, unthankful, unkind,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: without natural affection, implacable, false accusers, incontinent, fierce, not lovers of those who are good,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: traitors, heady, lofty, lovers of pleasure more than lovers of God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: having a form of piety, and its power having denied; and from these be turning away,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for of these there are those coming into the houses and leading captive the silly women, laden with sins, led away with desires manifold,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: always learning, and never to a knowledge of truth able to come,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and, even as Jannes and Jambres stood against Moses, so also these do stand against the truth, men corrupted in mind, disapproved concerning the faith;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but they shall not advance any further, for their folly shall be manifest to all, as theirs also did become.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And thou -- thou hast followed after my teaching, manner of life, purpose, faith, long-suffering, love, endurance,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the persecutions, the afflictions, that befell me in Antioch, in Iconium, in Lystra; what persecutions I endured, and out of all the Lord did deliver me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all also who will to live piously in Christ Jesus shall be persecuted,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and evil men and impostors shall advance to the worse, leading astray and being led astray.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And thou -- be remaining in the things which thou didst learn and wast entrusted with, having known from whom thou didst learn,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and because from a babe the Holy Writings thou hast known, which are able to make thee wise -- to salvation, through faith that [is] in Christ Jesus;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: every Writing [is] God-breathed, and profitable for teaching, for conviction, for setting aright, for instruction that [is] in righteousness,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that the man of God may be fitted -- for every good work having been completed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I do fully testify, then, before God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, who is about to judge living and dead at his manifestation and his reign --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: preach the word; be earnest in season, out of season, convict, rebuke, exhort, in all long-suffering and teaching,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for there shall be a season when the sound teaching they will not suffer, but according to their own desires to themselves they shall heap up teachers -- itching in the hearing,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and indeed, from the truth the hearing they shall turn away, and to the fables they shall be turned aside.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And thou -- watch in all things; suffer evil; do the work of one proclaiming good news; of thy ministration make full assurance,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I am already being poured out, and the time of my release hath arrived;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the good strife I have striven, the course I have finished, the faith I have kept,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: henceforth there is laid up for me the crown of the righteousness that the Lord -- the Righteous Judge -- shall give to me in that day, and not only to me, but also to all those loving his manifestation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Be diligent to come unto me quickly,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for Demas forsook me, having loved the present age, and went on to Thessalonica, Crescens to Galatia, Titus to Dalmatia,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lukas only is with me; Markus having taken, bring with thyself, for he is profitable to me for ministration;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Tychicus I sent to Ephesus;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the cloak that I left in Troas with Carpus, coming, bring thou and the books -- especially the parchments.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Alexander the coppersmith did me much evil; may the Lord repay to him according to his works,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: of whom also do thou beware, for greatly hath he stood against our words;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in my first defence no one stood with me, but all forsook me, (may it not be reckoned to them!)

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Lord stood by me, and did strengthen me, that through me the preaching might be fully assured, and all the nations might hear, and I was freed out of the mouth of a lion,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Lord shall free me from every evil work, and shall save [me] -- to his heavenly kingdom; to whom [is] the glory to the ages of the ages! Amen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Salute Prisca and Aquilas, and Onesiphorus' household;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Erastus did remain in Corinth, and Trophimus I left in Miletus infirm;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: be diligent to come before winter. Salute thee doth Eubulus, and Pudens, and Linus, and Claudia, and all the brethren.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The Lord Jesus Christ [is] with thy spirit; the grace [is] with you! Amen.
